rationale: det_clone_vec_u8_equal is defined as `r1 == r2` for r1,r2: Vec<u8>. The antecedent only provides view equality (r1@ == v@ && r2@ == v@), but Vec is registered via external_type_specification with external_body, so `==` is opaque structural equality at the SMT level. vstd has no axiom analogous to axiom_seq_ext_equal/axiom_set_ext_equal/axiom_map_ext_equal that lifts view equality to `==` on Vec. lemma_vec_obeys_view_eq only relates eq_spec (PartialEq) to view equality, not `==`. Tried assert(r1@ =~= r2@), conditional case split, broadcast use of lemma_vec_obeys_view_eq, and assert-by clauses — Verus successfully proves r1@ == r2@ but cannot bridge that to r1 == r2 (the assertion always fails at the structural Vec equality step).
